---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "split_h5mu"
namespace: "Dataflow"
description: "Split the samples of a single modality from a .h5mu (multimodal) sample into seperate .h5mu files based on the values of an .obs column of this modality."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `split_h5mu`  
Namespace: `dataflow`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/dataflow/split_h5mu)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/dataflow/split_h5mu)

:::





## Argument groups

### Input & specifications

|Name                        |Description                                                                            |Attributes                                |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`                   |Path to a single .h5mu file.                                                           |`file`, required, example: `"input.h5mu"` |
|`--modality`                |Which modality from the input MuData file to process.                                  |`string`, default: `"rna"`                |
|`--obs_feature`             |The .obs column to split the mudata on.                                                |`string`, required, example: `"celltype"` |
|`--drop_obs_nan`            |Whether to drop all .obs columns that contain only nan values after splitting.         |`boolean_true`                            |
|`--ensure_unique_filenames` |Append number suffixes to ensure unique filenames after sanitizing obs feature values. |`boolean_true`                            |


### Outputs

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                    |Attributes                                      |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output`             |Output directory containing multiple h5mu files.                                                               |`file`, required, example: `"/path/to/output"`  |
|`--output_files`       |A csv containing the base filename and obs feature by which it was split.                                      |`file`, required, example: `"sample_files.csv"` |
|`--output_compression` |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata H5 files. By default no compression is applied. |`string`, example: `"gzip"`                     |

## Authors


  * Dorien Roosen [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/dorien-er) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dorien-roosen) (author, maintainer)

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/dataflow/split_h5mu/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/dataflow/split_h5mu).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/dataflow/split_h5mu/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
